title: IAEA chief says Iran's nuclear sites will be inspected, Tehran bars access until final deal
content:
• International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) Director General Rafael Mariano Grossi stated that inspectors will eventually gain access to Iran’s uranium enrichment facilities.
• An interim agreement requires Iran to reduce its uranium stockpile through 'downblending' and for the United States to ease sanctions on Iran’s oil sector, with both sides working towards a broader agreement within a 60-day negotiating window.
• Technical-level negotiations are expected to resume next week at the Bürgenstock resort in Switzerland, while US Secretary of State Marco Rubio is on a three-country tour (United Arab Emirates, Kuwait, and Bahrain) in the Persian Gulf.

title: Airfares may climb up to 25% with doubling of fuel costs in 2026: Report
content:
• Airfares may climb by roughly 20 to 25 percent if fuel costs double, given that about 30 percent of an airline ticket price typically goes toward fuel.
• The jet fuel 'crack spread', historically around USD 20 per barrel or less, could average more than USD 50 per barrel in 2026.
• Major Gulf-region and Asian jet fuel exporters, which collectively supply 40 per cent of the world's jet fuel, have reduced refinery production, constraining supply.

title: Piyush Goyal to visit UK from 25-27 June to finalize trade deal
content:
• Commerce and industry minister Piyush Goyal will visit the UK from 25-27 June to finalize the India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA) and the Double Contribution Convention (DCC).
• The India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA) and the Double Contribution Convention (DCC) are expected to come into force on 15 July.
• India and the UK aim to expand bilateral trade between the two countries to $120 billion by 2030.

title: RBI tightens rules on recovery of excess pension payments
content:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has prohibited banks from recovering excess pension amounts from government employees without the pensioner's knowledge, consent, or a prior notice.
• The RBI has directed banks to obtain a written undertaking from pensioners at the time of account operation.
• This undertaking requires pensioners to refund any excess pension credited to their account upon receiving a suitable notice from the bank.

title: India, US inch closer to interim trade pact after fresh talks
content:
• Indian Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal and US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer concluded two days of negotiations aimed at finalising the first phase of a bilateral trade agreement (BTA).
• A crucial round of talks was held ahead of the July 24 expiry of 10% temporary tariffs by the US on imports from trading partners.
• US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer visited New Delhi from June 22-24 for talks, following chief negotiator-level discussions held in New Delhi from June 2-4.
